summary: Ship the agent built-in kind + named-field identity (the third mode): `.claude/agents/**/*.md`, id from the frontmatter `name` field, name+description required, the docs' name charset

new.description: Discovery folds `.claude/agents/**/*.md` (recursively) into agent members whose id is the frontmatter `name`, not the filename; the charset clause fires on an out-of-charset `name`; two agents sharing a `name` in one scope trip the uniqueness clause.

edit.description: Add the named-field identity mode ÔÇö a member's id read from a declared frontmatter field (agent's `name`), the third mode beside file-stem and directory-name (`UnitShape` enum, lines 111-119; `unit_shape_from_label` line 396 must round-trip the new label).

`Member::from_source` (line 152, and `from_source_rooted` line 171 where the id is derived) reads the id from the named frontmatter field for the named-field mode, not the file stem/parent directory; error when the field is absent (extend the `NoId` variant, lines 104-111, or a sibling).

Add `claude_code_agent()` beside `claude_code_command()` (line 86) ÔÇö Governs root `.claude/agents`, glob `**/*.md` (recursive, subdirs organizational), named-field identity on `name`, `Format::YamlFrontmatter`, `name`+`description` Field primitives, registration = {description-trigger}; add to `all_kinds()` (line 157); module doc (line 10) says 'four kinds' Ôćĺ five.

Spell the named-field identity mode in the `KindFacts` surface (interface line 53) ÔÇö the id source, not just `identityField`'s ordering/projection detail (lines 69-72); `UnitShape` union (line 19) gains the third mode or `identityField` becomes id-bearing.

Add the `agent` kind (locus `.claude/agents/**/*.md`, named-field identity, name+description) beside `command` (line 77) + `agentFloor`: required fields, `name` charset via `allowed_chars` ('lowercase letters and hyphens'), any closed vocabularies the docs document, per-scope name uniqueness ÔÇö each cited to code.claude.com/docs/en/sub-agents (2026-07-07).
Export `agent` and `agentFloor` ÔÇö join the export block (lines 12-25, alphabetical).
Regenerate ÔÇö the agent kind's declaration + floor rows join the lock (five kinds).
Assert the agent kind + its floor clauses re-derive (five kinds).
The frozen-lock byte-compare covers the regenerated five-kind lock.
Cover the agent kind + agentFloor (required fields, charset, uniqueness).

The `mod tests` built-in-kind assertions gain `agent` (five kinds): the name-list assert (line 53, `vec!["command","memory","rule","skill"]`) and the floor-clause match (line 62, `Some("command"|"skill"|"rule"|"memory")`).

Agent graduating to a built-in makes `.claude/agents` unconditionally governed, so its `KNOWN_SURFACES` row (lines 75-80) is permanently dead ÔÇö remove it (parallel to COMMAND-KIND dropping the `command` row, efd6caa). The gap-logic comment (line 139) uses `an agent kind rooted at .claude/agents` as the *locked custom kind* example ÔÇö retarget to a still-custom surface.

`a_present_surface_with_no_locked_or_builtin_governor_is_still_flagged` (~line 488) asserts `.claude/agents` under `builtin_set()` is flagged ungoverned ÔÇö false once agent is a built-in; retarget onto a surface still in `KNOWN_SURFACES` (`.mcp.json`/`.claude/settings.json`). `lock_agent_kind` + `a_locked_custom_kind_suppresses_the_surface_it_governs` (~lines 444-483) use `agent` as a custom-kind stand-in ÔÇö rewire onto a fresh not-yet-shipped kind name (agent no longer stands in, as command stopped at efd6caa).

tests.asserts: an agent's id is its frontmatter `name` (a file `foo.md` with `name: my-agent` Ôćĺ id `my-agent`); a subdirectory is organizational; an uppercase `name` trips the charset clause; duplicate `name`s in one scope trip uniqueness

acceptance: The agent kind ships: `.claude/agents/**/*.md` files fold recursively into agent members identified by the frontmatter `name` (the third identity mode), the built-in lock re-derives byte-clean, and `agentFloor` gates required fields, the documented `name` charset, and per-scope name uniqueness ÔÇö each clause cited to the sub-agents doc (builtins.md, 'The shipped kinds').
notes: 0014: agent kind + named-field identity (third mode), real engine work in from_source/UnitShape. Charset 'lowercase+hyphens', name+description required, cited sub-agents doc 2026-07-07; closed-vocab/uniqueness only where decidable. Ripple added: agent becoming a built-in kills coverage_note's .claude/agents KNOWN_SURFACES row + its still-flagged/lock_agent_kind tests + builtin_lock.rs name/floor asserts (cf COMMAND-KIND efd6caa). Five kinds. scoped 3e666a5.

summary: Ship the three acquisition channels ÔÇö SDK npm package, engine binary pinned via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone assets, the plugin/bundle ÔÇö fail-loud on a missing platform binary

gate.reason: needs the per-platform engine-binary release workflow build-out + John's decide-at-release calls (optional Apple notarization for the standalone mac binary, USPTO name screen) before the mechanical wrappers validate end-to-end. No marketplace/signing creds exist ÔÇö a marketplace is a git repo + marketplace.json, community submission a free web form gated by `claude plugin validate` (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs); npm creds done 07-05. SDK scope resolved (`@dtmd/temper` 0.0.4, single package, `(sdk-package-layout)`); channel 1's SDK foundation shipped. No `.github/workflows/release.yml` exists (only the `temper.yml` check job); root package.json is still the private flume manifest.

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages (channel 2: the engine binary pinned by the SDK via per-platform `optionalDependencies`, plus standalone GitHub release assets), and produce/publish the plugin bundle with its `marketplace.json` (channel 3). Channel auto-detected. Distinct from any CI *check* job, which is a documented two-line user-authored job (`temper check` + `emit --frozen` byte-compare), not a generated file (distribution.md).

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, distribution.md). Reconcile with the flume manifest rather than clobbering it. NB distinct from sdk/package.json (`@dtmd/temper`, already published ÔÇö the engine pin is declared there, once).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper via the SDK's pinned engine binary (per-platform optionalDependencies), standalone release assets, or the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip (distribution.md 'Fail-loud invariant').
notes: Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish; needs human creds. ch1 = SDK npm `@dtmd/temper`; ch2 = engine binary via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone; ch3 = plugin/bundle via `temper bundle` + marketplace.json. Parked on human release creds + the engine-binary workflow. install.rs range-correctness shipped in SCAFFOLD-OUTPUT-VALID (^0.0.4); release owns only future re-pins.
